name: Hfs
description_short: HFS Federal Credit Union is a Hawaii-based credit union serving residents of the Big Island, offering checking accounts, savings products, personal loans, and credit cards with competitive rates.
description_long:
HFS Federal Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial cooperative headquartered on the island of Hawaii. The institution serves a geographically defined membership base including those who live, work, worship, attend school, volunteer, or participate in associations on the Big Island. With physical branch locations in Hilo, Honoka'a, Kea'au, Kona Coast, Prince Kuhio Plaza, and Waimea, HFS provides traditional and digital banking services to its community.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products. These include checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs) with terms ranging from 6 to 60 months, personal loans with APRs from 5.75% to 10.00% depending on term length, the HFS Visa Platinum and Visa Signature credit cards, and auto loans. Members also benefit from educational partnerships like the Zogo financial literacy platform and receive promotional offers such as cash back rewards on credit card purchases and referral bonuses.

HFS differentiates itself through its localized community focus and member-centric branding (referring to its community as the "'ohana"). The credit union emphasizes accessibility with digital banking capabilities, provides competitive dividend rates on share certificates (6-month term at 3.56% APY as of April 2026), and maintains a legacy narrative highlighting member stories. Their routing number (321378660) and established branch network demonstrate operational stability.

As a credit union, HFS operates under member ownership rather than shareholder profit motives, which typically results in competitive rates and lower fees. However, membership eligibility is geographically restricted to Hawaii residents and those with qualifying connections to the Big Island, limiting accessibility compared to national banks. The institution appears financially sound but serves a niche regional market rather than offering nationwide reach.
services: Checking accounts with online and mobile banking access
Savings accounts with $50 minimum opening balance
Share Certificates (CDs) with terms from 6 to 60 months
Personal loans with terms up to 60 months and APRs from 5.75%-10.00%
HFS Visa Platinum credit card (12.25% APR)
HFS Visa Signature credit card (2% cash back, 30,000 CURewards points promotion)
Zogo financial literacy platform with educational modules and rewards
Digital account management and online banking
Branch services at six Big Island locations
pros: Share Certificate rates competitive at 3.56% APY for 6-month terms (as of April 2026)
Personal loan APRs start at 5.75% with automatic payment discount, competitive for unsecured lending
Member-owned credit union structure typically offers lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
Six physical branch locations across Big Island for in-person service and local accessibility
Digital banking capabilities allowing seamless account management online and via mobile
Refer-a-friend program offering $25 rewards for both referrer and new member
Educational partnerships including Zogo financial literacy platform with incentive programs
cons: Membership restricted to those living, working, or studying on Hawaii's Big Island—no mainland or nationwide access
Limited geographic footprint with only 6 branches, all located on single island
Share Certificate rates display as blank on website (rates effective April 5, 2026 not fully visible), making current rate comparison difficult
Credit card APR of 12.25% on HFS Visa Platinum is relatively standard, offering no advantage over major competitors
Personal loan maximum APR reaches 10.00% for longer terms, which is moderate to high for unsecured lending
best_for: Hawaii Big Island residents seeking local, community-focused banking with personalized service
Those looking for competitive CD rates and savings products with credit union benefits
Members interested in auto financing and personal loans with reasonable APRs and local branch access
Communities wanting to support local financial institutions and member-owned cooperative banking models
diagnosis: HFS Federal Credit Union is best suited for residents of Hawaii's Big Island seeking traditional banking services with competitive rates and local community focus. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility—those not living, working, or studying on the Big Island cannot join, making this institution unavailable to most U.S. consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
